files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index edb7f9d..ae81daf 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -110,2 +110,9 @@ Day 105 / Week 15 observations:
 - Usage peaked around 1.24M/day, still under tier-3 capacity. S2 issue aging is low, but S1 issue aging is creeping up; social is now mostly positive with an onboarding/documentation complaint.
 - Week 15 adjustment: start tier-2 R&D, hold the proven S2 growth/support/dev settings steady to rebuild cash after the one-time spend, and post about onboarding templates.
+
+Day 112 / Week 16 observations:
+- After funding tier-2 R&D, cash still ended at $257k. Excluding the one-time research cost, weekly operations would have added roughly $174k.
+- S2 content remained very strong: 1,304 paid content subscribers from 4,037 leads, including 426 B and 781 C.
+- Usage peaked around 1.49M/day, still below tier-3 capacity. Tier 4 is not needed yet, but headroom should be watched if acquisition scales again.
+- Open issue volume rose to 770. S2 issue age is still below half a day on average, but support-related social posts about critical bugs/radio silence resurfaced.
+- Week 16 adjustment: keep ads/prices/dev/capacity stable while tier-2 R&D runs, increase S2/S1 support coverage, and post a concrete critical-bug status-update commitment.
